title: stachys officinalis subsp. velebitica
intro: Stachys officinalis subsp. velebitica, also known as Velebitian betony, is a subspecies of the betony plant, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Balkan Peninsula, particularly in the Velebit Mountains of Croatia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and wounds. The medicinal properties of Stachys officinalis subsp. velebitica are often harnessed through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which provide relief from symptoms and promote overall well-being.
